refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly focus on the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, clarifying the performance target and scope.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation scenarios; verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: The system shall minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: The system shall monitor and report real-time latency metrics to the operator and safety services.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize network protocol stack and data compression to reduce latency.
DP2: Implement dedicated low-latency communication channels with QoS guarantees.
DP3: Integrate real-time latency monitoring and adaptive control to maintain latency targets.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Implement dedicated low-latency communication channels with QoS guarantees.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication channel and vehicle control systems
Dependency on network infrastructure and safety services
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates latency control in communication channels, minimizing coupling with other DPs.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can support QoS guarantees.
Risk of latency spikes due to unforeseen network congestion.
Assumes telemetry data is accurate and timely.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms in case QoS guarantees fail.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for implementing communication channels, latency optimization, and telemetry reporting for teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network interface for data exchange with remote operator and vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send latency and status metrics to Safety Services and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-related functions; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle module depends on teleoperation data but does not control latency mechanisms.
Receives latency alerts but does not implement latency control.
Coordinates teleoperation but latency control is delegated.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control and monitoring are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize cross-module diffusion and simplify ownership.
containment_risks: Potential latency issues caused by vehicle hardware or network infrastructure outside Teleoperation control.
Dependency on accurate telemetry data from multiple modules.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts and monitoring responsibilities with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (dedicated low-latency communication channels with QoS) directly addresses the refined FR sub-requirements with clear performance targets.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as latency control is centralized in Teleoperation module.
Some risk of cross-module dependencies with Vehicle and Safety Services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate for latency control.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry interface and network link monitoring within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control DP to Teleoperation IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback and alert mechanisms for latency violations.
Clarify telemetry data accuracy and synchronization requirements.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to fallback mechanisms and telemetry accuracy require addressing before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: The system shall minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: The system shall monitor and report real-time latency metrics to the operator and safety services.

revise.why: Incorporate fallback mechanisms and adaptive controls to handle QoS failures and network variability.

assumptions: Network conditions can support QoS guarantees.
Telemetry data is accurate and timely.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network congestion.
evidence_needed: Network performance test results under agreed conditions.
